commands.review_summary:
The branch adds shared outbound account selection after channel resolution, wires it into runMessageAction, updates routing/message docs, and adds focused resolver/plugin-dispatch tests.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main leaves omitted accountId unresolved in runMessageAction, and WhatsApp then falls through to default-account resolution even when the reported config has disabled default and one enabled named account.
commands.review_followup: The patch is a normal review candidate rather than an automated repair: the remaining blockers are contributor real-behavior proof and maintainer acceptance of the fail-closed upgrade behavior.

target.title: fix(outbound): auto-select single enabled message account
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #20756.

- Add a shared outbound message account resolver after channel selection.
- Preserve explicit `accountId`, CLI/default account overrides, and agent binding account IDs before inference.
- When the message tool omits an account, prefer an enabled configured default account; otherwise auto-select exactly one enabled configured account and fail clearly for zero or multiple choices.
- Document the omitted-account behavior in the channel routing and message CLI docs.

No changelog entry is included because contributor-authored PRs should leave changelog credit/addition to maintainers during landing.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/channel-selection.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.plugin-dispatch.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/send.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/active-listener.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/message.channels.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.media.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.poll.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/infra/outbound/channel-selection.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-selection.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.plugin-dispatch.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/send.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/active-listener.test.ts docs/channels/channel-routing.md docs/cli/message.md`
- `pnpm docs:check-mdx docs/channels/channel-routing.md docs/cli/message.md`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm check:changed`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: omitted message-tool `accountId` no longer falls through to a disabled `default` account when the selected channel has exactly one enabled configured account.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on Windows 11, branch `hemant/fix-message-account-selection`, using the repo Vitest wrapper and local changed-check gate.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: the verification commands listed above were run after formatting the patch.

Evidence after fix: focused resolver and plugin-dispatch tests pass, including the regression where `default` is disabled and `xiaomi` is the only enabled configured account; docs MDX, formatting, diff whitespace, and `check:changed` all exit successfully.

Observed result after fix: the shared runner writes the inferred account into both the plugin action context and normalized params before dispatch; ambiguous multi-account configs fail before dispatch with an explicit account-selection error.

What was not tested: live WhatsApp delivery was not run. The remote Crabbox/Testbox wrapper could not start in this environment because its binary failed its own sanity check; direct `pnpm tsgo:core` was also blocked locally by an existing Windows path-with-space invocation issue, while `pnpm check:changed` completed successfully.
target.author: hemantsudarshan
